title: zig-zone: private mesh + workshop/server split via Tailscale
description:
## 1. Overview

`zig-zone` is the user's private mesh — a Tailscale tailnet wrapping 5 devices into one virtual LAN regardless of physical network:

- `zig-computer` — Ubuntu VPS, public IP `51.81.33.136`, OVH KVM
- `pico` — Mac Studio M1 Max 64GB, headless, on home LAN
- `metis` — personal MacBook
- `zig` — work MacBook (subject to OQ-03 — work device policy)
- iPhone (Tailscale iOS app + Termius SSH client)

**The split this spec enables.** zig-computer becomes the **workshop + edge**: it stays the user's primary coding box (Claude Code, dotfiles, agent harness) AND retains the public surface — but the public surface narrows to just three things: nginx (TLS terminator + reverse proxy for everything), sshd on :22 (break-glass), and tailscaled. **All hostable services move to pico**: Ollama (LLM inference), `vs14-web`, the SS14 game server, the vacation-station postgres dbs, the Docker observability stack, AND `reef-router` (the granola webhook listener). nginx on zig-computer reverse-proxies inbound HTTP/UDP/TCP through the tailnet to pico. All inter-machine SSH happens primarily via Tailscale SSH.

**pico is a fresh install.** It has macOS but no `mac.setup.sh` has run, no Homebrew, no dotfiles cloned, no Tailscale. The other devices (zig-computer, metis, zig, iPhone) are already configured and only need Tailscale-bring-up + ACL alignment. So this spec includes a one-time **pico bootstrap** section (prerequisites) before the mesh phase.

**Why.** The user wants the workshop machine free from interruption — services on the same box as your active development creates risk of accidental kills, port conflicts, resource pressure mid-flow. The Mac Studio is purpose-built for sustained server load (silent, efficient, Apple Silicon GPU for inference). Plus: the M1 Max's 64GB unified memory gives a real local-LLM serving option that the OVH x86 VM can't match.

**In scope.** Pico fresh-install bootstrap (prerequisite), mesh bootstrap, ACL, the 5-phase service migration from zig-computer → pico (including reef-router), the dotfiles changes to install + configure Tailscale + Ollama on both OSes, the zig-zone skill/runbook.

**Out of scope.** LoRA fine-tuning workflow (deferred to a future spec — MLX-LM + Python venv on pico). Headscale migration (future). Subnet routing (no other home-LAN devices need exposure). Additional VPS/devices. Tailscale Funnel (we have a VPS w/ public IP already).

**Sources informing this spec.**
- Three Explore-agent research briefs run 2026-05-23 covering: Tailscale fundamentals + ACLs + SSH, VPS-as-edge w/ nginx stream{} forwarding, Mac Studio LLM stack (Ollama/MLX/LM Studio)
- `~/explore/.claude/skills/zig-computer-audit/SKILL.md` — current machine topology, services, firewall posture
- Live audit run 2026-05-23: 34 active services on zig-computer, UFW deny-default w/ 6 allow rules, fail2ban 2,573 total bans / 29 active, public ports 22/80/443/1212/1213/7575
- Tailscale docs: tailscale.com/kb (MagicDNS, ACLs, SSH, exit nodes, subnet routers, control-vs-data-plane)
- SS14 docs: docs.spacestation14.com (port-forwarding, hub registration)
- Ollama remote-access patterns: launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST, Tailscale-IP binding

## 2. Current State / Baseline

### 2.1 Network topology (today)

```
Internet
   ↓
zig-computer (VPS, 51.81.33.136)  ← all services live here
   • SSH :22 (LIMIT, fail2ban-protected) ← all 3 personal devices SSH here directly
   • nginx :80/:443 (linearb.zig.computer, ss14.zig.computer)
   • reef-router :7575 (granola webhook)
   • SS14 game server :1212/:1213 UDP+TCP
   • ss14-watchdog.service
   • vs14-web.service (127.0.0.1:3300, fronted by nginx)
   • postgresql@17-main.service (127.0.0.1:5432 + 172.17.0.1:5432 for docker)
   • Docker: 5 vs14 obs containers (grafana, loki, prometheus, ss14-admin, mapserver)
   • ollama.service (127.0.0.1:11434)
   • phoenix :6006 + :4317 (LLM observability)
```

pico exists but is not yet on any shared network. metis + zig + iPhone independently SSH to zig-computer over the public internet via port 22.

### 2.2 UFW state (verified 2026-05-23)

```
22/tcp           LIMIT  Anywhere       (OpenSSH)
80,443/tcp       ALLOW  Anywhere       (Nginx)
1212             ALLOW  Anywhere       (SS14 live)
1213             ALLOW  Anywhere       (SS14 dev)
7575/tcp         ALLOW  Anywhere       (reef-router/granola)
44880            ALLOW  172.18.0.0/16  (prometheus, docker-bridge scoped)
5432/tcp         ALLOW  172.17.0.0/16  (postgres, docker-bridge scoped)
```

Default deny incoming, allow outgoing, deny routed.

### 2.3 Dotfiles baseline

- `mac.setup.sh` line 5: `brew install tmux jq gh ranger ... dicklesworthstone/tap/bv` — NO `ollama`, NO `tailscale`
- `ubuntu.setup.sh`: no Tailscale install
- No `tailscale/` dir
- No zig-zone runbook
- No Ollama plist (correct — Homebrew + `brew services` handles it)

### 2.4 Why the change

The user wants:
- Light, durable, non-locked-in mesh networking (Tailscale w/ Headscale exit option preserved)
- Workshop/server discipline (coding box ≠ service box)
- Local LLM inference at zero marginal cost via pico's Apple Silicon GPU
- Reach pico from anywhere (laptops in cafés, phone) via mesh, not by port-forwarding the home router
- Eventually run longer-interval agentic loops against pico (free model, no quota pressure)

## 3. Changes and Decisions

| Decision | Why | How |
|---|---|---|
| **Adopt Tailscale free tier** (not Headscale day-one) | Easy onboarding, peer-to-peer data plane preserves privacy, Headscale is the exit ramp if ever needed (drop-in compatible). 5 devices fits the 100-device limit comfortably. | `curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh \| sh` (Ubuntu); `brew install tailscale` (macOS); App Store (iOS). `sudo tailscale up` triggers browser SSO. |
| **MagicDNS on** | `<host>.<tailnet>.ts.net` names work transparently across the mesh. SSH/nginx configs become trivial. | Default-on; tailnet DNS name picked in admin console. |
| **Tailscale SSH = primary path; sshd port 22 = break-glass** | Tailscale-only is the #1 day-one mistake on remote VPS (coordination-server lockout risk). Current sshd posture (LIMIT + fail2ban: 2,573 historic bans, 29 currently banned) is doing real work. | Enable `tailscale up --ssh` on zig-computer + pico. ACL grants SSH to `tag:user-devices`. Keep `ufw limit 22/tcp` as is. |
| **No subnet router** | User has no other home-LAN devices that need tailnet exposure right now. One-line change to add later if a NAS/Pi appears. | Skip `--advertise-routes` flag on pico. |
| **No exit node** | Not needed — VPS already has public IP; user isn't trying to hide origin or geo-shift. | Skip `--advertise-exit-node` on any device. |
| **Migrate all hostable EXTERNAL services zig-computer → pico** | Workshop/server discipline. M1 Max is a better server for everything except the public-edge role. | Phased (§ 4.4). |
| **Keep on zig-computer: nginx + sshd-break-glass + tailscaled + dev tooling (tmux, lb-fleet, lb-listener)** | nginx is the public TLS terminator and DNS landing zone; sshd stays as Tailscale-down recovery; tailscaled is the mesh client. tmux + lb-fleet + lb-listener are localhost-bound dev tools that belong with the workshop role — not user-facing services. | Don't touch these in migration; only re-point nginx upstreams to pico tailnet IPs. |
| **Migrate reef-router to pico** | The granola webhook is just an HTTP endpoint; reef-router (bun process) runs fine on macOS; nginx-on-zig-computer can `proxy_pass http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:7575` for it. Keeps zig-computer's "workshop + pure edge" discipline intact. Latency is fine for webhook traffic (granola is async/eventual). | Phase 4 (with the other HTTP services). The public UFW rule for :7575 stays — nginx listens on it now and forwards through the tailnet. Granola's webhook URL doesn't change. |
| **Ollama on pico binds to tailnet IP only** | Default `0.0.0.0` would expose the API to any home-LAN guest; `127.0.0.1` would block tailnet access. Tailnet-IP-only is the precise scope. | `launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST "$(tailscale ip -4):11434"` before `brew services start ollama`. |
| **SS14 migration is conditional** | UDP forwarding via nginx stream{} adds 10-20ms + jitter; SS14 may embed bind address in handshakes (untested per OQ-01). | Phase 4 begins with a feasibility test; spec defines abort criteria if test fails. |
| **Dotfiles additions stay light** | User's explicit preference. | New: `tailscale/acl.json`, `tailscale/README.md`, `agents/skills/zig-zone/SKILL.md`. Modified: `mac.setup.sh` (4 lines added), `ubuntu.setup.sh` (1 line added). |

## 4. Formal Specification

### 4.1 Device naming (tailnet hostnames = OS hostnames)

| Device | Tailnet name | MagicDNS | OS | Role |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| OVH VPS | `zig-computer` | `zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net` | Ubuntu 25.10 | workshop + edge |
| Mac Studio M1 Max 64GB | `pico` | `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` | macOS | server farm |
| Personal MacBook | `metis` | `metis.<tailnet>.ts.net` | macOS | client |
| Work MacBook | `zig` | `zig.<tailnet>.ts.net` | macOS | client (subject to OQ-03) |
| iPhone | (iOS device name, lowercased) | `<phone>.<tailnet>.ts.net` | iOS | client via Termius |

Tailnet DNS name itself (the `<tailnet>` part) is decided in the Tailscale admin console — see OQ-04.

### 4.2 Tailscale ACL (`tailscale/acl.json`)

```jsonc
{
  // Single-user personal tailnet.
  //
  // Identity model:
  //   - `zig-computer` and `pico` are tagged `tag:server` (machine identities,
  //     no human user behind them).
  //   - `metis`, `zig`, and the iPhone are UNTAGGED — they auth as the user's
  //     Tailscale account, so they match `autogroup:member` (human-owned devices).
  //   - This separation matters: `autogroup:member` in Tailscale ACLs refers to
  //     human-owned untagged devices; tagged devices are NOT autogroup:member.
  //     Mixing the two requires explicit listing in src/dst.

  "tagOwners": {
    "tag:server": ["autogroup:admin"]   // zig-computer, pico
  },

  "acls": [
    // Server-to-server (e.g., nginx-on-zig-computer → vs14-web-on-pico via tailnet)
    { "action": "accept", "src": ["tag:server"], "dst": ["tag:server:*"] },
    // User devices to servers (SSH, Ollama API, vs14-web preview from a laptop, etc.)
    { "action": "accept", "src": ["autogroup:member"], "dst": ["tag:server:*"] },
    // User devices among themselves — use `autogroup:self` (the user's own devices)
    // because `autogroup:member` is src-only in Tailscale ACLs and would error as dst.
    // `autogroup:self` matches devices owned by the SAME user as the source — i.e.,
    // metis ↔ zig ↔ iPhone for the user's personal-account devices.
    { "action": "accept", "src": ["autogroup:member"], "dst": ["autogroup:self:*"] }
  ],

  "ssh": [
    // Tailscale SSH: user devices (metis, zig, iPhone) → server devices (zig-computer, pico).
    // `check` action requires Tailscale-identity re-auth every 12h.
    {
      "action": "check",
      "src":    ["autogroup:member"],
      "dst":    ["tag:server"],
      "users":  ["ubuntu", "autogroup:nonroot"]
    }
  ]

  // No exit nodes, no subnet routes — re-evaluate post-Phase-5.
}
```

**Tags applied per-server only.** zig-computer and pico get `--advertise-tags=tag:server` in their respective Phase 1 `tailscale up` (NOT during fresh-install bootstrap — see § 4.3 note). The user devices stay untagged and naturally match `autogroup:member` once they auth as the user's Tailscale account.

**Why this shape over `["*"]` wildcards.** Explicit src/dst pairs make the policy reviewable — if a future device joins untagged AND unauthenticated, no rule matches it and it's denied by default. A `["*"]` rule would silently grant.

### 4.3 Dotfiles changes

**New files:**

```
dotfiles/tailscale/
├── acl.json         # source of truth for the Tailscale admin policy
└── README.md        # topology + recovery procedures (mirrors zig-zone skill)

dotfiles/agents/skills/zig-zone/
└── SKILL.md         # runbook — auto-loaded as /zig-zone
```

**Modified files:**

`dotfiles/mac.setup.sh` — line 5 gains `ollama tailscale colima docker docker-compose`; new lines after:

```bash
# zig-zone: tailscale + ollama bring-up
# (intentionally does NOT include --advertise-tags or --ssh here — those wait
#  until Phase 1, AFTER the ACL with tagOwners has been pasted into Tailscale
#  admin. Pre-tag advertise fails with "requested tags not permitted" on a
#  fresh tailnet. Phase 1 step 3 re-runs `tailscale up` with the tag flag.)
sudo pmset -a sleep 0 displaysleep 0 disksleep 0 powernap 0
sudo pmset -a autorestart 1                              # auto-boot after power loss
sudo softwareupdate --schedule off                       # no surprise reboots
sudo tailscale up                                        # plain join — tags + ssh come in Phase 1
launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST "$(tailscale ip -4):11434"
brew services start ollama
```

`dotfiles/ubuntu.setup.sh` — add:
```bash
# zig-zone: tailscale (plain join — tags + ssh come in Phase 1)
curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh
sudo tailscale up
```

**No new `launchd/` dir.** `brew services start ollama` manages its own LaunchAgent; `OLLAMA_HOST` is provided to that agent via `launchctl setenv`.

**Docker runtime: Colima, not Docker Desktop.** Colima is OSS, headless-friendly (no GUI requirement), and works with the standard `docker` CLI. Docker Desktop on Mac requires the GUI app running and has licensing nuance. For an unattended server, Colima is the right pick. The Mac bridge networking pattern is `host.docker.internal` (works on both Colima and Docker Desktop) — see § 4.4 Phase 3 for the postgres-bridge migration step.

### 4.4 Migration phases

Each phase has explicit acceptance criteria (§ 5 test cases). A phase isn't done until its tests pass on the live system.

#### Prerequisites — pico fresh-install bootstrap + one-time safety checks (one-time)

pico is a fresh macOS install. Before any mesh work happens, pico needs to BE a functional server with the dotfiles environment, AND a few one-time safety checks need to happen on the other devices.

**0. One-time safety verifications (do these BEFORE pico bootstrap):**

  - **OVH KVM console access on zig-computer**: log into OVH manager, navigate to the VPS, click "KVM console," see the Ubuntu login prompt, log out. Confirms the nuclear-recovery path works AND that you know where the OVH credentials live (NOT in dotfiles — store separately, e.g. password manager). If KVM doesn't launch (Java applet, browser quirks, 2FA on a missing phone) — resolve NOW, before any change to public-SSH posture.
  - **macOS settings on pico (before mac.setup.sh runs):**
    - **FileVault**: in System Settings → Privacy & Security → FileVault, **turn OFF** (or accept that pico is unreachable for any reboot until physically present to enter password). For an in-home server, threat model justifies off.
    - **Auto-restart after power loss**: System Settings → Energy → "Start up automatically after a power failure" → ON. mac.setup.sh later adds `sudo pmset -a autorestart 1` as belt-and-suspenders.
    - **Disable macOS auto-install of updates**: System Settings → General → Software Update → Advanced → uncheck "Install macOS updates" and "Install system data files and security updates." mac.setup.sh adds `sudo softwareupdate --schedule off` as the CLI equivalent.

**1. pico bootstrap proper:**

1. Enable Remote Login (System Settings → General → Sharing → Remote Login → ON) so we can SSH in from the home LAN to drive the rest
2. Install Xcode Command Line Tools: `xcode-select --install` (interactive GUI dialog opens; let it finish before continuing)
3. Install Homebrew (the canonical bootstrap one-liner from brew.sh)
4. Clone `~/dotfiles`: `git clone https://github.com/azigler/dotfiles ~/dotfiles`
5. Run `bash ~/dotfiles/mac.setup.sh` — now includes `ollama` + `tailscale` + `colima` + `docker` + `docker-compose` in the brew line, plus the pmset + softwareupdate + plain-`tailscale up` + launchctl-setenv-OLLAMA_HOST + brew-services-start-ollama lines (see § 4.3). The full `brew install` line runs as-is even though some packages (yabai, skhd, borders, sketchybar) are UI tools that won't be useful on a headless box — acceptable for now; cleanup is a future polish, not a Prerequisites blocker.
6. Run `bash ~/dotfiles/sync.sh` (or equivalent) to symlink configs into `$HOME`
7. Verify Homebrew installed everything: `brew list | grep -E "ollama|tailscale|colima"`
8. Start Colima: `colima start` (creates the Linux VM that Docker runs in). Verify `docker info` works.
9. `mac.setup.sh` already ran `sudo tailscale up` (plain — no tags, no SSH). Browser SSO prompt opens; auth as the user's Tailscale account; pico joins the tailnet.

**Acceptance:** OVH KVM verified working; FileVault off / auto-restart on / auto-update off; pico can SSH-receive (LAN); `mac.setup.sh` ran clean; `brew services list` shows ollama (binding deferred until Phase 2); `colima status` shows running; `tailscale status` shows pico joined (untagged, no SSH yet — both come in Phase 1).

#### Phase 0 — Mesh bootstrap (no service changes)

1. Pre-generate one Tailscale auth key per device in the admin console (or use browser SSO)
2. Install Tailscale on all 5 devices (`zig-computer`, `pico`, `metis`, `zig`, iPhone)
3. `sudo tailscale up` on each (no SSH yet, no tags yet — just join the tailnet)
4. Verify `tailscale status` on each device lists all 5 peers
5. Verify MagicDNS: `ping pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` from `zig-computer` resolves
6. Verify connection type: `tailscale status` shows `direct` between `zig-computer` and `pico` (NOT `relay` — flag for investigation if so)

**Reversible:** `sudo tailscale down` on each, uninstall. No service changes yet.

#### Phase 1 — Hardening (ACL + tags + SSH enablement)

1. Commit `tailscale/acl.json` + `tailscale/README.md` to dotfiles
2. Paste `tailscale/acl.json` into Tailscale admin → Access Controls → Save. Verify policy compiles (Tailscale's editor shows red-underline on errors).
3. Re-run on the servers: `sudo tailscale up --ssh --advertise-tags=tag:server` on `zig-computer`; same on `pico`. (NOW the tag is permitted because `tagOwners` exists in the ACL.)
4. User devices DON'T get tags. On `metis`, `zig`, iPhone: re-run `tailscale up --ssh` (or just `tailscale set --ssh=true`) to enable Tailscale SSH from those devices to the servers. They naturally match `autogroup:member` once authenticated.
5. Test (TC04): `tailscale ssh ubuntu@zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net` from `metis` succeeds
6. Test: `tailscale ping zig-computer` from `pico` succeeds (server-to-server reachability)
7. Test (TC05): existing `ssh ubuntu@51.81.33.136` on port 22 still works (break-glass intact)

Per OQ-02 (decided): leave `ufw limit 22/tcp` as-is. No IP allowlist tightening.

#### Phase 2 — Ollama + Phoenix migration

Ollama + Phoenix move as one wave to keep trace data co-located with the model that produces it (no cross-tailnet trace traffic).

1. On `pico`: `brew install ollama` (if not yet), `launchctl setenv OLLAMA_HOST "$(tailscale ip -4):11434"`, `brew services start ollama`
2. `ollama pull llama3.2:3b` (smoke-test model) + `ollama pull qwen2.5:7b` (real model)
3. On `pico`: install Phoenix (`pip install arize-phoenix` or `uv pip install arize-phoenix`), start it bound to tailnet IP, gRPC on the same `:4317` it uses today
4. From `zig-computer`: `curl http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:11434/api/tags` returns 200 with model list
5. From `zig-computer`: `curl http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:6006` returns the Phoenix UI
6. On `zig-computer`: `sudo systemctl stop ollama lb-phoenix && sudo systemctl disable ollama lb-phoenix`
7. Verify on `zig-computer`: `curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags` returns connection-refused; `curl http://localhost:6006` returns connection-refused
8. Update any client config (Claude Code, agentic loops, OTEL env vars `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`) to point at `http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:11434` and `http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:4317` respectively

#### Phase 3 — vs14-web + postgres + observability containers + reef-router + project timers

All HTTP/TCP-only services in one wave. They share the migration pattern: stop on zig-computer, run on pico bound to the tailnet IP (per OQ-05 decision), point nginx upstream at `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:<port>`, **wait for in-flight connections to drain (`sleep 60`)**, then stop the old.

1. **Postgres**: on `pico`, `brew install postgresql@17`, `brew services start postgresql@17`. **Postgres is the explicit exception to OQ-05** — it stays bound to `127.0.0.1` (Homebrew default), NOT to the tailnet IP, because the only consumers (vs14-web, mapserver container via host.docker.internal) are co-located on pico. TC11 verifies this is intentional, not an oversight. From zig-computer, dump both DBs: `pg_dump -h localhost -U <user> vacation_station > /tmp/vs.sql` + same for `vacation_station_mapserver`. Transfer via tailnet: `scp /tmp/*.sql ubuntu@pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:/tmp/`. On pico: `createdb vacation_station vacation_station_mapserver`, `psql vacation_station < /tmp/vs.sql`, same for mapserver. Also recreate any roles/passwords (`pg_dumpall --roles-only`) and adjust `pg_hba.conf` on pico to match access policy. **Step 1 must complete before step 2** — mapserver container in step 2 connects to this postgres.
2. **Docker via Colima**: on `pico`, `colima start` (if not already). Transfer the 5 obs container compose files + data volumes from zig-computer (`scp -r ~/vacation-station-14/docker/* ubuntu@pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:~/vacation-station-14/docker/` — adjust paths to actual layout). Verify volume data integrity.
3. **Critical postgres-bridge fix**: on Linux, the mapserver container connected to host postgres at `172.17.0.1:5432` (docker0 bridge gateway). **Colima on macOS does NOT have `172.17.0.1`.** Update the mapserver container's connection string env (`DATABASE_URL` or equivalent) from `172.17.0.1:5432` → `host.docker.internal:5432`. Verify from inside the container: `docker exec mapserver psql -h host.docker.internal -U <user> -d vacation_station_mapserver -c '\dt'`.
4. **Project timers** (8 known per audit skill § 6 "Periodic timers worth knowing"):

   | Timer | Purpose | Suggested launchd schedule |
   |---|---|---|
   | `ss14-backup.timer` | Daily Postgres backup of vacation_station | daily 04:00 |
   | `ss14-replay-rotate.timer` | Rotate game replays | daily 03:30 |
   | `vs14-postgres-retention.timer` | Postgres retention sweep | daily 04:30 |
   | `vs14-cookbook-build.timer` | Build cookbook content | weekly Sunday |
   | `vs14-guidebook-build.timer` | Build guidebook content | weekly Sunday |
   | `vs14-nurseshark-build.timer` | Build nurseshark content | weekly Sunday |
   | `vs14-writer-build.timer` | Build writer content | weekly Sunday |
   | `vs14-map-render.timer` | Render maps | weekly Sunday |

   Confirm the exact `OnCalendar=` schedules with `systemctl cat <timer>` before porting — the table is a starting point, not authoritative.

   **launchd translation cost**: macOS `launchd` does NOT have a direct `OnCalendar=daily` equivalent. The `StartCalendarInterval` key takes a dictionary per fire-time (or an array of dictionaries for multiple times). Budget extra time per timer for the translation; `cron` is the lighter-weight alternative if scheduling is simple (daily/weekly at a fixed time). Port each timer's underlying script to pico first, then schedule. Disable on zig-computer once the pico equivalents are running: `sudo systemctl disable --now <list-of-timers>`.
5. **reef-router**: on `pico`, `git clone <reef-remote-url> ~/linearb/reef` (it IS a real remote-tracked repo — verify with `git -C ~/linearb/reef remote -v`). Then `cd ~/linearb/reef && bun install`. **Transfer secrets out-of-band**: scp `~/linearb/reef/.env.local` AND `~/linearb/reef/polyps/router/.env.local` from zig-computer to pico (they're gitignored; clone doesn't carry them). Create launchd plist at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.zig.reef-router.plist` mirroring the systemd unit (WorkingDirectory, ExecStart=bun run polyps/router/src/index.ts, EnvironmentVariables including `REEF_HOST=$(tailscale ip -4)`, EnvironmentFile equivalents via launchd's plist syntax). `launchctl load` it.
6. **vs14-web**: on `pico`, clone the vs14 repo (or scp it), `npm install` / `bun install` as appropriate. **Verify Tailscale is up first** — `tailscale ip -4` should return a `100.x.y.z` address; if empty, run `sudo tailscale up` and retry. Then start `next start -H $(tailscale ip -4) -p 3300`. Verify via tailnet from zig-computer: `curl -I http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:3300`.
7. **nginx upstream updates on zig-computer**: edit each vhost so `proxy_pass` → `http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:<port>` for vs14-web. Add a new server block listening on `:7575` (both ipv4 and ipv6) that `proxy_pass http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:7575` for the granola webhook. Granola's webhook URL doesn't change; UFW already allows :7575.
8. `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx`
9. **Verify** (TC10 + TC17): `https://linearb.zig.computer/<known-vs14-route>` resolves and renders content sourced from pico; `curl -X POST http://<public-ip>:7575/<webhook-path>` reaches reef-router on pico (check reef-router logs on pico for the receipt).
10. **Drain window**: `sleep 60` to let in-flight HTTP/2 streams + WebSocket connections finish on the old upstreams.
11. On `zig-computer`: `sudo systemctl stop vs14-web postgresql@17-main reef-router && sudo systemctl disable vs14-web postgresql@17-main reef-router`. Stop + remove the 5 obs containers from zig-computer's docker.

#### Phase 4 — SS14 game server (conditional on feasibility test)

**Cut-over must happen in a known no-players window** — UDP streams don't drain cleanly via nginx, and any current player gets disconnected instantly. Schedule for low-activity time.

**Performance baseline (recommended before this phase starts):** play-test the current SS14 server on zig-computer for ~10 minutes; note subjective latency / smoothness. After Phase 4 step 5, play-test the pico-hosted version and compare. If pico-via-tailnet feels equivalent or better, the architecture is validated.

1. On `pico`: install SS14 server + watchdog into `/opt/ss14-watchdog/` per upstream docs (will likely need a launchd plist instead of systemd; mirror the watchdog config)
2. Configure SS14 to bind `0.0.0.0:1212` (test the simpler bind first; switch to tailnet-IP if leaked-address test fails)
3. On `zig-computer`: write nginx stream{} config:
   ```nginx
   stream {
       upstream ss14_udp { server pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:1212; }
       server { listen 1212 udp reuseport; proxy_pass ss14_udp; proxy_timeout 30s; }
       upstream ss14_tcp { server pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:1212; }
       server { listen 1212; proxy_pass ss14_tcp; }
   }
   ```
4. `sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx`
5. **Feasibility test:** stop the SS14 game server on `zig-computer`; have a real client connect to `ss14.zig.computer:1212` (or the public IP); play for 5+ minutes; on the CLIENT machine, run `tcpdump -n host pico.<tailnet>.ts.net or 'src net 100.0.0.0/8'` and verify NO packets target tailnet hostnames or IPs (any such packets would indicate SS14 leaked its bind address — see OQ-01)
6. **If test passes:** proceed — disable `ss14-watchdog.service` on `zig-computer`, leave the nginx stream{} forwarding live, port the `ss14-backup.timer` and `ss14-replay-rotate.timer` to launchd on pico
7. **If test fails:** abort SS14 migration. SS14 stays on `zig-computer`. The nginx stream{} block is removed. Update spec with findings (file `ss14-migration-failed` note bead). Other migrations (Ollama, Phoenix, vs14-web, etc.) are independent and stand.

#### Phase 5 — Done state verification

Final state asserted by tests TC14 + TC15 (§ 5). On `zig-computer`:

**System services running**: edge surface (nginx, sshd, fail2ban), system services (systemd-*, dbus, cron, ufw, unattended-upgrades, qemu-guest-agent, etc.), tailscaled.

**User services running (dev tooling — explicit "stays here"):** `tmux.service` (keeps the user's tmux session alive), `lb-fleet.service` (lb-agent-fleet dev server on 127.0.0.1:7100 — internal/localhost), `lb-listener.service` (lb-agent-listener dev server on 127.0.0.1:7102 — internal/localhost). These are NOT external services and stay on zig-computer as part of the workshop environment. **Future consideration**: revisit moving lb-fleet / lb-listener to pico in a later spec once the user's workflow patterns stabilize — both are currently localhost-bound dev tooling, not user-facing servers, so the workshop/server discipline applies less strictly.

**NOT running on zig-computer**: `ss14-*`, `vs14-*`, `postgresql@*`, `ollama`, `reef-router`, `lb-phoenix`, vs14 docker workload containers. Public ports 1212/1213/7575 are now handled by nginx + nginx stream{} proxying to pico.

**Dual-stack edge preserved.** zig-computer's nginx still listens on `[::]:443` and `0.0.0.0:443` (and ditto for 80, 7575); IPv6 inbound traffic terminates at the edge and the tailnet upstream connection to pico is v4-only via `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` (Tailscale assigns `100.x.y.z` v4 addresses by default). No DNS-record changes needed.

### 4.5 Failure conditions + recovery

| Failure | Symptom | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| Tailscale coordination server down | New SSH sessions via `tailscale ssh` fail to authenticate | Fall back to `ssh ubuntu@51.81.33.136` (LIMITed sshd is intact); existing sessions stay live |
| pico tailscaled crashes / Mac reboots | nginx upstream `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` unreachable; vs14-web + SS14 + Ollama all 502/timeout | `brew services restart tailscale` on pico (or full reboot — LaunchDaemon brings it back); nginx upstream auto-recovers on next request |
| Home ISP outage / CGNAT relay fallback | Latency to pico spikes from 5ms → 30ms+; SS14 players notice; Ollama still works but slower | No fast fix; ride out outage. If chronic, investigate Tailscale DERP relay region selection or ISP change |
| OVH VPS rebooted / public IP changes | DNS still points at old IP; all public traffic dead | OVH KVM console; check `ip addr`; update DNS A records if IP changed (rare) |
| Locked out entirely (Tailscale + sshd both unreachable) | Can't reach `zig-computer` at all | OVH KVM web console (documented in zig-zone skill); break-glass user/password reset path |
| Auth key compromise | Unknown device joins tailnet | Tailscale admin console: revoke key, audit log shows affected devices, force re-auth |

## 5. Test Cases (≥10)

```
TEST: TC01-mesh-status
INPUT:    `tailscale status` on each of 5 devices
EXPECTED: each output lists the other 4 devices with hostname, IP (100.x.y.z), connection type
RATIONALE: confirms mesh bootstrap succeeded for all peers

TEST: TC02-magicdns-resolve
INPUT:    `dig +short pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` from zig-computer
EXPECTED: returns pico's 100.x.y.z tailnet IP
RATIONALE: MagicDNS is the lookup substrate every later config depends on

TEST: TC03-direct-connection
INPUT:    `tailscale status` on zig-computer, grep for pico
EXPECTED: line shows `direct` (not `relay`)
RATIONALE: relay fallback would multiply latency 4-10x — flag for ISP/NAT investigation

TEST: TC04-tailscale-ssh-from-laptop
INPUT:    `tailscale ssh ubuntu@zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net` from metis
EXPECTED: shell prompt, no password/key prompt, identity proven via Tailscale
RATIONALE: validates Tailscale SSH is the primary path; ACL policy is correct

TEST: TC05-break-glass-ssh-still-works
INPUT:    `ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ubuntu@51.81.33.136` from metis (with the existing ~/.ssh/id_*) using port 22
EXPECTED: succeeds (LIMITed sshd still accepts)
RATIONALE: confirms the break-glass survives Tailscale enablement

TEST: TC06-fail2ban-still-active
INPUT:    `sudo fail2ban-client status sshd` on zig-computer
EXPECTED: jail status active, banned count nonzero, journal matches updated within last 24h
RATIONALE: existing brute-force protection is the safety net for break-glass exposure

TEST: TC07-ollama-on-pico-tailnet-only
INPUT:    `lsof -iTCP:11434 -sTCP:LISTEN` on pico
EXPECTED: shows only one bind on the tailnet IP (100.x.y.z), NOT 0.0.0.0 and NOT 127.0.0.1
RATIONALE: misbound Ollama either leaks to home LAN guests (0.0.0.0) or blocks tailnet clients (127.0.0.1) — both wrong

TEST: TC08-ollama-reachable-from-vps
INPUT:    `curl -s http://pico.<tailnet>.ts.net:11434/api/tags` from zig-computer
EXPECTED: HTTP 200, JSON body with `models` array containing the pulled models
RATIONALE: validates the canonical inference path end-to-end

TEST: TC09-old-ollama-gone
INPUT:    `curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags` on zig-computer; `systemctl is-enabled ollama.service`
EXPECTED: connection-refused; `disabled`
RATIONALE: no two Ollama processes; agentic clients can't accidentally hit the old endpoint

TEST: TC10-vs14-web-via-tailnet
INPUT:    `curl -sI https://linearb.zig.computer/<known-vs14-route>` from anywhere on internet
EXPECTED: HTTP 200, Server: nginx, content originates from pico
RATIONALE: confirms nginx-on-zig-computer → tailnet → vs14-web-on-pico works for public HTTPS traffic

TEST: TC11-postgres-not-on-tailnet
INPUT:    `nc -zv pico.<tailnet>.ts.net 5432` from zig-computer
EXPECTED: connection refused (postgres on pico binds 127.0.0.1 only)
RATIONALE: postgres has no need to be tailnet-reachable — vs14-web on pico hits it via loopback

TEST: TC12-ss14-feasibility (Phase 4 gate)
INPUT:    real client connects to ss14.zig.computer:1212; play session for 5 minutes; tcpdump on the client for any packets to *.ts.net hostnames
EXPECTED: gameplay smooth, no client→tailnet-hostname packets observed
RATIONALE: validates SS14/Robust Toolbox doesn't leak its bind address — the load-bearing OQ-01 question

TEST: TC13-iphone-ssh-via-termius
INPUT:    on iPhone, open Tailscale iOS app + verify "Connected" status; open Termius; connect to `ubuntu@zig-computer.<tailnet>.ts.net:22` (plain SSH — Termius does NOT natively speak Tailscale SSH protocol, but plain SSH via the tailnet IP works because the iPhone is on the mesh)
EXPECTED: shell connects, prompts for SSH key passphrase if applicable
RATIONALE: confirms iOS device is fully usable on tailnet via plain SSH over the WireGuard tunnel. Document expected backgrounding behavior: Tailscale iOS may suspend after extended battery-pressure backgrounding → SSH session drops; reconnect by re-opening Tailscale app. Works equivalently on cellular vs WiFi (Tailscale routes both).

TEST: TC14-zig-computer-end-state
INPUT:    `systemctl list-units --state=running --no-pager` AND `systemctl --user list-units --state=running --no-pager` on zig-computer (post-Phase-5)
EXPECTED:
  System services: contains nginx, sshd, fail2ban, tailscaled; NO entries matching `ss14-*`, `vs14-*`, `postgresql@*`
  User services: contains tmux.service, lb-fleet.service, lb-listener.service (all dev tooling — explicit "stays"); NO entries matching `lb-phoenix`, `reef-router`, `ollama`, or vs14 docker containers
RATIONALE: validates workshop/server split. The dev-tooling exceptions (tmux, lb-fleet, lb-listener) are deliberately documented as staying — they're localhost-bound internal tools that belong with the workshop role, not external services.

TEST: TC17-reef-router-webhook-via-tailnet
INPUT:    `curl -sS -X POST http://51.81.33.136:7575/<webhook-path> -d '<test-payload>'` from external
EXPECTED: HTTP 200 / 2xx, payload reaches reef-router on pico (verify via reef-router logs on pico)
RATIONALE: confirms granola webhook still works end-to-end with nginx-on-zig-computer reverse-proxying to pico; latency budget for webhook traffic is generous

TEST: TC18-pico-bootstrap-complete
INPUT:    on pico, `brew list | grep -E "ollama|tailscale"`; `tailscale status`; `ls ~/dotfiles/mac.setup.sh`
EXPECTED: all three return success (ollama + tailscale installed; pico in tailnet; mac.setup.sh present)
RATIONALE: prerequisites complete — confirms pico is a functional server before any Phase begins

TEST: TC15-acl-policy-deny-unrelated
INPUT:    in Tailscale admin "policy editor" preview tool, simulate src = a future contractor's device (NOT on the user's account, NOT tagged), dst = `pico:22`
EXPECTED: policy denies (no rule matches — autogroup:member only matches devices owned by the user's account; tags are server-side and explicit)
RATIONALE: confirms ACL is allowlist-default-deny — a stray device on the tailnet (e.g., a co-owner's account added later, or a contractor) gets no access without explicit grant. Replaces the earlier `tag:client` formulation (that tag no longer exists in the rev-2 ACL — user devices are untagged and use autogroup:member).

TEST: TC16-headscale-key-portability (smoke)
INPUT:    `tailscale netcheck`; review private key location on a Mac (~/Library/Application Support/Tailscale/) and Linux (/var/lib/tailscale/)
EXPECTED: keys are local files; documented in zig-zone skill as part of recovery
RATIONALE: confirms not-locked-in property; if Tailscale Inc. ever becomes hostile, keys can move to Headscale
```

## 6. Open Questions

- **OQ-01 (P1): Does SS14 / Robust Toolbox engine embed its bind address in network handshakes or hub-advertise calls?**
  - DECIDED 2026-05-23: test-driven — Phase 4 step 5 is the feasibility test. **Abort criterion**: if any client packets target `*.ts.net` hostnames during the 5-minute play session, SS14 stays on zig-computer permanently (or moves only via a different mechanism — WireGuard direct, FRP, etc. — out of scope for this spec). The other phases (Ollama, Phoenix, vs14, postgres, obs, reef-router) are independent and complete regardless of SS14 outcome.
  - Affects: Phase 4 (decisive, but isolated)

- **OQ-02 (P1): Is your home/office public IP stable enough to allowlist on port 22?**
  - DECIDED 2026-05-23: keep `ufw limit 22/tcp` + fail2ban (current state). No IP allowlist. Existing posture catches enough (2,573 bans / 29 currently banned) and doesn't depend on a stable home IP. Phase 1 step 8 is dropped.
  - Affects: Phase 1 simplified

- **OQ-03 (P2): Does the work laptop (`zig`) allow installing Tailscale per your employer's device-management policy?**
  - DECIDED 2026-05-23: yes, allowed. `zig` joins the tailnet as `tag:client` like `metis`. Tailscale SSH works from both.
  - Affects: ACL roster confirmed at 5 devices

- **OQ-04 (P2): What tailnet DNS name should we pick?**
  - DECIDED 2026-05-23: aspirational target = `zig-zone.ts.net`. If taken when the user is in the admin console, fall back to whatever's available in Tailscale's picker. Spec keeps `<tailnet>.ts.net` placeholder syntax in all URLs until the actual name is locked.
  - Affects: cosmetics only; the spec doesn't change behavior either way

- **OQ-05 (P2): Should the HTTP services on pico (vs14-web, reef-router) bind to `127.0.0.1:<port>` or to pico's tailnet IP?**
  - DECIDED 2026-05-23: Option B — direct tailnet-IP bind. Same pattern as Ollama's `OLLAMA_HOST=$(tailscale ip -4):11434`. No local nginx on pico. Tailnet is the trust boundary.
  - Affects: Phase 3 confirmed

- **OQ-06 (P3): Phoenix observability (`*:4317` gRPC on zig-computer — currently UFW-protected) — does it migrate to pico in Phase 3, or stay on zig-computer?**
  - DECIDED 2026-05-23: migrates to pico **in Phase 2** alongside Ollama (option "with Ollama"). Keeps Ollama traces local to pico — no cross-tailnet trace traffic per request. Phase 2 description updated to include Phoenix bring-up steps.
  - Affects: Phase 2 scope expanded

- **OQ-07 (P3): Should we set up Tailscale's HTTPS cert for `pico.<tailnet>.ts.net` (`tailscale cert`) so internal traffic VPS→pico is TLS-encrypted on top of WireGuard?**
  - DECIDED 2026-05-23: NO. WireGuard already encrypts the underlying link. Belt-and-suspenders with no real threat-model benefit. Revisit only if a future app demands HTTPS upstream.
  - Affects: nothing day-one

## 7. Future Considerations

- **Move lb-fleet + lb-listener to pico (or retire them)** — currently localhost-bound dev tools on zig-computer. If the user's workflow shifts toward agentic loops running entirely on pico (with the workshop just sending prompts), these tools become more useful on pico than on zig-computer. Defer until workflow patterns stabilize. Out of zig-zone Phase 5 scope.
- **Fine-tuning workflow** — separate spec. Add MLX-LM + a Python venv on pico, LaunchDaemon for `mlx_lm.server` if we want a second always-on inference endpoint, dotfiles entry for the venv requirements. Estimated complexity: small spec, ~3 phases.
- **Headscale migration** — if Tailscale Inc. ever changes pricing/policy. The dotfiles work is mostly the same; only the coordination server URL changes. Devices keep their WireGuard keys; you redirect them at `headscale.zig.computer` (a service on zig-computer's edge nginx).
- **Subnet routing** — if a NAS/Pi/Home Assistant box appears on the home LAN, advertise `192.168.x.0/24` via `tailscale up --advertise-routes`. One-line change.
- **Exit node** — if you want to route phone/laptop traffic through home for geo or trust reasons. `tailscale up --advertise-exit-node` on pico (or zig-computer).
- **Additional VPS** — if you want geographic redundancy on the edge, a second VPS with its own public IP joining the tailnet as `corsair` (different cloud, different country). nginx vhosts mirror.
- **Tailscale Funnel** — if you want to expose a single pico service publicly without going through zig-computer (rare — you have zig-computer for that). Per-service decision.
- **Per-service ACL refinement** — current ACL is full mesh among autogroup:member. If you ever add a household member or a contractor to the tailnet, the ACL becomes more granular per `tag:server`/`tag:client` and per-port.
- **Audit logging** — Tailscale paid tier offers audit logs. Free tier doesn't. If audit becomes important, that's the upgrade trigger.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] Section 1.4 sources documented (audit skill, 3 research briefs, live audit run, Tailscale/SS14/Ollama docs cited)
 - [x] Sections 2 and 3 cite existing code/services by reference (UFW state verified, mac.setup.sh line 5 cited, current systemd inventory matches audit skill Phase 6)
 - [x] Section 5 has 16 test cases (TC01-TC16) each with INPUT/EXPECTED/RATIONALE
 - [x] Section 6 lists 7 open questions, each with priority + recommendation
 - [x] Each migration phase has explicit acceptance criteria mapped to TC# in section 5
 - [x] Failure conditions + recovery documented (section 4.5)

---
## Post-spec rule addition — 2026-05-24 (bead dotfiles-qix)

ACL ssh section was extended with a second rule allowing tag:server → tag:server SSH (zig-computer ↔ pico) — discovered during Phase 1 testing as a real workflow gap. The user lives on zig-computer (workshop) and needs to reach pico for ops without a laptop hop. /scrutinize rounds reviewed the spec's documented intent (client→server) and didn't flag the server→server omission because it wasn't in the documented requirements; this came up the first time the user actually tried it.

Same security posture as the existing client→server rule: `check` action (12h re-auth), users restricted to autogroup:nonroot. Captured in:
- ACL file: ~/dotfiles/tailscale/acl.jsonc (commit 32ea167)
- Runbook: ~/explore/.claude/skills/zig-zone/SKILL.md "Daily ops" section (explore commit 488f473)

The spec's stated principle stands; this is a refinement under it, not a supersession.

---
## Post-spec architectural finding — 2026-05-24 (Phase 1)

**Tag**: this changes a load-bearing assumption in the spec without changing the user-facing intent. Not a supersession; an implementation constraint discovered late.

**Finding**: spec § 4 assumed both pico and zig-computer could run as Tailscale SSH servers AND have MagicDNS for outbound queries. **On macOS, this is fundamentally impossible.** The three Tailscale install variants are mutually exclusive on this feature pair:
- brew formula (CLI/headless tailscaled): SSH server YES, MagicDNS NO (userspace networking)
- Mac App Store: SSH server NO (sandboxed), MagicDNS YES
- "Standalone" .pkg / brew cask: SSH server NO (also sandboxed, despite the naming), MagicDNS YES

**Why /scrutinize missed it**: 3 rounds reviewed the spec's documented intent ("Tailscale SSH is the primary inter-machine SSH path") but didn't actually install the brew formula and try to use MagicDNS outbound from the same host. The constraint is operational, not a spec-stated requirement.

**Resolution** (in scope; doesn't break any spec section, refines § 4):
- pico uses the brew FORMULA → Tailscale SSH server works (clients ssh INTO pico via Tailscale identity, ACL-gated)
- pico's outbound MagicDNS works around with the /etc/hosts shim + the `ssh-zig` / `ssh-pico` aliases that bypass DNS via `tailscale ip -4 <host>` (bead dotfiles-tc1 added the aliases to ~/dotfiles/bash/.bash_aliases)
- For future workstation Macs (metis-style): use cask/App Store variant — opposite trade-off but right for client role

**Documentation trail**:
- Runbook gotcha #12: ~/explore/.claude/skills/zig-zone/SKILL.md (explore commit b1881d6)
- Runbook gotcha #13 (stale /usr/local/bin shims after Standalone uninstall): same commit
- mac.setup.sh comment block: ~/dotfiles/mac.setup.sh (commit 2e2965f)
- SSH aliases: ~/dotfiles/bash/.bash_aliases (commit 15a19b4, bead dotfiles-tc1)

Spec § 4.4 Phase 1 "Tailscale SSH from any device" intent stands; the implementation is per-role variant + workaround for the server side.

title: hooks: pre-commit-checks Bead-trailer carve-out misses :outbox_tray: (distribute)
description:
## Problem
pre-commit-checks.sh's Bead-trailer block (shipped in dotfiles-5q1)
exempts meta-commits by gitmoji — :card_file_box: / :broom: / :dollar:
— but misses :outbox_tray:, the gitmoji /distribute uses for
harness-sync commits. A `:outbox_tray: distribute: sync from dotfiles`
commit has no owning bead and is wrongly blocked.

## Symptom
Caught immediately: the /distribute sync commit to ~/linearb/skills
this session is blocked by the hook just shipped in dotfiles-5q1.

## Cause
The carve-out gitmoji alternation was incomplete — :outbox_tray: was
overlooked when enumerating meta-commit classes.

## Fix
Add :outbox_tray: to the carve-out alternation; cover it with a test
case. (This is the "carve-out must be maintained" cost the hooks
analysis flagged for the Bead-trailer block.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] :outbox_tray: distribute commits exempt from the Bead-trailer block
- [ ] test-pre-commit-checks.sh covers it and stays green
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] :outbox_tray: added to the carve-out; distribute commits pass
 - [ ] test-pre-commit-checks.sh covers it and is green

Move these 7 to pico:

title: tailscale/acl.jsonc: add tag:server → tag:server SSH rule (server-to-server gap)
description:
## Problem
Our `ssh` ACL allows only `autogroup:member` (clients: metis, iPhone) → `tag:server` (zig-computer, pico). It does NOT allow `tag:server` → `tag:server` SSH, so server-to-server Tailscale SSH is denied.

Real workflow gap: the user spends much of their time SSH'd into zig-computer (the workshop). From there, they want to `tailscale ssh <user>@pico` for ops/debug without bouncing back to a laptop. Today: `tailnet policy does not permit you to SSH to this node`.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. From a `tag:server` device (e.g., zig-computer), run `tailscale ssh <valid-user>@pico.tailfb4637.ts.net`
2. Observe: `tailscale: tailnet policy does not permit you to SSH to this node`

Expected: connection succeeds (with `check` re-auth) and drops you in pico's shell.

## Root cause
Spec said "all inter-machine SSH happens primarily via Tailscale SSH" but ACL was tighter — only documented the client-side path. /scrutinize rounds reviewed the spec's intent (client→server) and didn't flag the server→server gap because it wasn't in the documented requirements.

## Fix
Append one rule to the `ssh` ACL: `tag:server` → `tag:server`, `check` action (12h re-auth), users = `autogroup:nonroot` (no root, no exotic system users). Update comment block accordingly.

## Regression guard
- Update the `/zig-zone` runbook (in explore) to document the server→server SSH rule and the workflow it enables
- Append a `## Decision` block to the closed spec bead `dotfiles-phe` --notes — promotes the rule from gap-discovered to documented-design

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] `tailscale/acl.jsonc` has a second ssh rule: tag:server → tag:server, check action, users=autogroup:nonroot
- [x] User re-pastes ACL into Tailscale admin
- [x] `tailscale ssh pico@picos-mac-studio` from zig-computer succeeds after the check re-auth
- [x] `/zig-zone` runbook in explore notes the rule
- [x] dotfiles-phe --notes appended with the decision pointer
